claude.question: What are the key allegations and current status of the O'Melveny & Myers investigation report regarding Rick Caruso's handling of the USC George Tyndall sexual abuse scandal that he promised to release?
claude.answer:
Based on my research, here is a comprehensive overview of the O'Melveny & Myers investigation regarding Rick Caruso's handling of the USC George Tyndall scandal:

## Background and Promise to Release

USC hired the law firm O'Melveny & Myers to conduct an internal investigation into Tyndall and USC's administration to uncover who knew about Tyndall's activities. Caruso promised to make the findings of the investigation publicly available. However, this did not happen. In May 2018, Caruso promised an independent investigation and an accompanying public report, but no report on the investigation has been made public.

## Key Allegations Within the Investigation

The investigation itself addressed fundamental questions about institutional responsibility. The O'Melveny & Myers' report could reveal which USC officials knew about the allegations against Tyndall, yet went ahead and authorized the doctor's quiet resignation. The report would reveal who is to blame for the unchecked sexual abuse and harassment that damaged so many young women students and cost the university at least $1.1 billion in settlements.

During Caruso's deposition in October 2020, Caruso turned to attorney-client privilege repeatedly when declining to disclose information on administrative discussions regarding Tyndall or the conclusions of the investigation of Tyndall's three-decade-long abusive history at USC. Lawyers from the O'Melveny & Myers law firm engaged in some of the most controversial lines of questioning in the entire testimony.

## Current Status of the Report

As of the available information, the report remains unreleased despite Caruso's initial promises. Though Caruso promised reporters in 2018 and 2019 to release these questions to the public, he has yet to do so. In April 2019, Caruso told the LA Times that the investigation was still not complete, but said, "My intent and promise was that the board and university would be transparent and I plan on living up to that promise." Today, Caruso is less clear. He did not answer specific questions about the Tyndall investigation, nor did he repeat his earlier promises to release as much of it as possible.

## Justification and Victim Criticism

Caruso, who is running to be mayor of Los Angeles, said in a debate with opponent and Congressmember Karen Bass that his reason for withholding the findings was to protect the victims of Tyndall. Caruso stated, "My deposition is completely consistent with what I've said, is I wanted to give out as much information as possible. The decision was made not to, based on the experts we had, not to re-victimize these students."

However, this justification has been strongly disputed by victims and their attorneys. Attorney Gloria Allred said many clients' major goal in pursuing justice was to know why USC failed to protect them. Allred said Caruso promised an investigation upon which the finished report would be released to the public and the victims. "He raised the hopes of victims, who simply wanted the truth," Allred said. "But then he cruelly broke his promise to them later, leaving many of them feeling betrayed by him and the university they represented."

claude.question: How has Rick Caruso's political party affiliation and voter registration status changed between 2011 and 2026, and what explanations has he provided for these shifts?
claude.answer:
Based on my research, here is a comprehensive analysis of Rick Caruso's political party affiliation and voter registration changes between 2011 and 2026:

## Voter Registration Timeline and Shifts

Rick Caruso, who was a Republican for much of his life, switched his party registration to "decline to state" in 2011 while mulling over a run in the city's 2013 mayoral election. This marked the beginning of a dramatic series of political realignments over the subsequent fifteen years. Caruso re-registered as a Republican in March 2016 and kept his Republican Party affiliation until mid-November 2019, when he changed his registration back to "no party preference." Caruso changed his party affiliation to Democratic in late January of this year [2022], less than a month before filing to run in the 2022 mayoral election. In total, the real estate developer has changed his party registration four times in the last 11 years, shifting twice between Republican and no party preference before re-registering as a Democrat about four months ago (as of June 2022).

## Explanations Provided by Caruso

When questioned about his frequent party switches, Caruso has offered several explanations that frame his shifts as ideological consistency rather than opportunism. He became an independent because he didn't like ideology, saying "When I was a Republican, I was a centrist. When I was an independent, I was a centrist. As a Democrat, I'm a centrist," describing himself as "fiscally conservative but socially accepting of how people want to live their lives." Regarding his 2022 shift to the Democratic Party, Caruso stated "For more than a decade I have been registered to vote with no party preference -- I have been a true political centrist -- because neither the Democratic nor the Republican parties captured what I believed we needed to do as a nation." 

He wrote in a letter that "I am registering as a Democrat so that I can stand firmly on the side of the fundamental values that we will all need to invoke and enforce to thwart the coming attacks on our democracy." When directly confronted about his history of registration changes during his mayoral campaign, Caruso said "I'm a Democrat, and I'm proud to be a Democrat" and stated "What fascinates me is that we're even having this conversation because the electorate, the voters in the city don't care about this. What the voters of the city care about is crime and homelessness and corruption."

## Regarding His 2016 Republican Re-Registration

Shortly after changing his registration in 2016, Caruso told Los Angeles Magazine that he had made the switch so that he could vote for John Kasich in the Republican presidential primary that year, and served as Kasich's California co-chair while being outspoken about his dislike for then-candidate Donald Trump. This explanation suggests his brief return to Republican registration was motivated by a specific primary election preference rather than a sustained political realignment.

## Critical Reception

Despite Caruso's explanations, his political fluidity has drawn significant criticism. Caruso's opponents and Democratic officials argued that his frequent switches suggested either a lack of core principles or opportunistic positioning. His explanations emphasizing centrism and consistency on fundamental values were largely dismissed by critics who pointed to his historical donations to Republican candidates and his recent conversion to the Democratic Party in the heavily liberal city of Los Angeles as evidence of calculated political maneuvering rather than genuine philosophical evolution.

claude.question: What were the primary financial details and public reactions to Rick Caruso's $100+ million personal spending on his 2022 Los Angeles mayoral campaign against Karen Bass?
claude.answer:
## Rick Caruso's $100+ Million Spending on the 2022 Los Angeles Mayoral Campaign

### Financial Details of the Campaign

Rick Caruso spent $104,848,887.43 on the mayor's race through November 2, while Karen Bass spent $9,060,958.59, making this the most expensive mayoral race in Los Angeles history. He outspent Bass by nearly 11-to-1 in the four-month campaign, riding waves of TV and digital ads to run neck-and-neck in the weeks leading up to the general election. The disparity in spending sources was striking: the vast majority of the money in this year's race came from one source: mayoral candidate Rick Caruso's bank account.

The efficiency of Caruso's spending tells a cautionary tale about the return on his investment. In the most expensive race in Los Angeles history, Rick Caruso spent $160.42 per vote while Karen Bass spent $10.15 to become L.A. mayor. When accounting for votes across both the primary and general elections, the disparity was even more pronounced: Caruso's total per vote spent comes to $338.42, while for Bass, the total is $21.94. Additionally, Bass collected 14,227 separate contributions, while Caruso received 3,101 donations.

### Public Reactions and Campaign Impact

Despite the massive spending advantage, public reactions revealed significant skepticism about money-driven campaigns. After spending nearly $100 million in the Los Angeles mayoral campaign, Rick Caruso rapidly caught up to his rival Karen Bass, putting the race in a statistical tie. However, this near-parity proved short-lived. Rick Caruso conceded the mayoral election in Los Angeles to U.S. Rep. Karen Bass, a longtime elected official who withstood his $100 million self-funded campaign to pull away to a handy victory after a close campaign.

The outcome sparked broader commentary about the limits of wealth in politics. Los Angeles might not have another candidate willing to dump $100 million of their own money into the race the next time the mayor's office is up for grabs. But it's unlikely the city will ever see a race where the campaign for the top office costs less than $10 million either. "The losing side always decides that next time they need to spend even more, so these trends are only going to continue to escalate," said Schnur. The campaign also raised questions about the relationship between spending and electoral success, with analysts noting what's still unclear is whether the vast amounts of money spent—mostly by Caruso—on get-out-the-vote efforts moved the needle as well.

claude.question: What specific CEQA-related litigation has Rick Caruso's Caruso real estate company initiated or funded since 2024, and what are the legal arguments and outcomes?
claude.answer:
Based on my search findings, I can provide a detailed analysis of the CEQA-related litigation involving Rick Caruso's real estate company since 2024.

## The Television City CEQA Lawsuit: Caruso's Single Major Initiative Since 2024

In 2025, one of Caruso's malls filed a CEQA suit seeking to block the $1 billion Television City project in the Fairfax District of Los Angeles. The company that manages The Grove—the roughly 600,000-square-foot luxury mall owned by Caruso in L.A.'s Fairfax District—filed a lawsuit against the city of L.A. and studio developer and owner Hackman Capital Partners (HCP) earlier this month, claiming that the city's approval of the neighboring Television City studio expansion project in early January violated the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA). The suit ultimately seeks to overturn the city's decision.

## Legal Arguments and Violations Claimed

Alleged CEQA violations revolve around claims that the project's environmental impact review misled the public. "The Project has changed many times in scope and uses in a way that is impossible for the public to follow and renders the project description unstable," writes Benjamin J. Hanelin, a lawyer for The Grove, in the lawsuit, which also claims violations of housing and zoning laws. More specifically, the lawsuit alleges the city violated not just CEQA, but also state housing law and Measure HLA, which requires bus and bike lanes on certain corridors. The complaint argues that the ambiguity makes it difficult to accurately measure the project's environmental effects, with the City Council showing "stunning disregard" for CEQA regulations in its approval. According to Caruso's thinking, the expansion is a "speculative real estate venture" rather than an effort to address a shortage of soundstages since additional production space isn't guaranteed to be built, and points to Hackman's plans not requiring a studio to be built or maintained on the property and allowing for a wide range of development that it neglected to describe.

## Case Status and Defendants' Response

The lawsuit calls for a judge to block Television City and overturn city approvals and an environmental impact report. As of the available information, the case has not yet been resolved. Hackman Capital Partners Senior Vice President Zach Sokoloff said in a statement that "These coordinated lawsuits are an unfortunate but predictable abuse of CEQA to stop the Television City studio plan," pointing to the lengthy review process the project underwent to gain approvals from the city, including revisions in 2024, and noting that "at every step of the City process, the TVC Project was unanimously approved."

## The Irony of Caruso's Position

Notably, this litigation represents a significant departure from Caruso's previously stated positions on CEQA. Over the years, Caruso has been a vocal critic of the weaponization of CEQA to block development projects. When running for mayor in 2022, he said he'd mandate the disclosure of payments made by labor unions and environmental groups that regularly claim a violation of the law to "unfairly and disingenuously leverage development projects." As part of his 2022 mayoral campaign, Caruso pledged to require a $15,000 application fee for CEQA complaints (specifically in regard to housing projects) in order to "reduce frivolous CEQA challenges that impede sensible development." This lawsuit against Television City thus represents what critics view as a strategic use of the very legal mechanism Caruso had previously criticized as a barrier to development.

claude.question: What press coverage and community response has Rick Caruso received regarding his January 2026 withdrawal from political races and his February 2026 reconsideration announcement?
claude.answer:
Based on my search results, I can provide you with a comprehensive overview of the press coverage and community response to Rick Caruso's January 2026 withdrawal announcement and his February 2026 reconsideration.

## January 2026 Withdrawal Announcement and Press Coverage

Rick Caruso announced on January 16, 2026, that he will not run for office. His decision received significant media attention across major news outlets. Political expert Zev Yaroslavsky at the UCLA Luskin School of Public Affairs described the move as "surprising" in a statement to CBS News Los Angeles. Yaroslavsky mulled over the possibility that Caruso may have recognized the challenges incumbent in winning a contested race either on the city level or statewide.

In his public announcement, Caruso stated, "It is a difficult decision, and I am deeply disappointed to step back from an election I believe is so critical to California's future." Caruso emphasized that "where I can make the greatest impact, and how a possible campaign for public office would affect my family" were the two questions that guided his thinking. He committed to focus on wildfire recovery through his nonprofit Steadfast LA.

## Community Response to the Withdrawal

Community reaction to Caruso's withdrawal announcement was mixed. Comments in local press outlets reflected diverse perspectives from his supporters. Some expressed disappointment at his decision, with one commenter noting he was their "only hope for mayor," while others appreciated his prioritization of family considerations. The withdrawal announcement was widely covered by local and national media outlets, including NBC Los Angeles, Bloomberg, FOX, and multiple community news organizations.

## February 2026 Reconsideration Announcement

The political situation shifted unexpectedly in early February. Rick Caruso began reconsidering his decision to enter the 2026 mayoral election following reports of Bass alerting the Palisades after-action report. Rick Caruso indicated he was reconsidering his decision not to run in 2026.

When speaking about his earlier withdrawal, Caruso stated, "I was eager and ready to run for governor and excited about that, about what I could do, but in the scheme of things and a lot of talk with the family and reflection with the family, it was just best that now is not the right time." Notably, although Caruso's name wasn't on the ballot initially, he said he planned to continue to call out elected officials he disagrees with, noting "I might even be more unfiltered now."

## Caruso's Post-Withdrawal Public Profile

Despite his initial decision not to run, Caruso maintained a high-profile public presence. Caruso remained busy on the homefront, overseeing the rebuilding process of the Palisades Recreation Center and preparing for the 2026 reopening of his shopping center, Palisades Village. His pivot toward reconsideration in February reflected evolving political circumstances and continued engagement with municipal affairs, particularly regarding criticism of Mayor Bass's handling of the wildfire recovery.

claude.question: What are the documented financial contributions and involvement of Rick Caruso with Steadfast LA or similar political organizations, and what policy positions do these affiliations represent?
claude.answer:
Based on my research, I can provide a comprehensive answer about Rick Caruso's financial contributions and political involvement, particularly regarding Steadfast LA and his broader political activities.

## Steadfast LA: Structure and Financial Involvement

Steadfast LA is a nonprofit dedicated to rebuilding efforts led by businessman and former mayoral candidate Rick Caruso, established in response to the January 2026 Los Angeles wildfires. However, it's important to clarify that Steadfast LA is primarily a disaster relief and rebuilding organization rather than a traditional political organization. Caruso is funding Steadfast LA entirely himself so the effort doesn't compete for resources, and is working with the public and private sectors to kick the rebuilding process into high gear. Caruso stated he plans to raise $30 million around the initiative, which will probably provide about 80 to 100 homes, and then grow it from there with other home providers and other home builders.

## Policy Positions Represented by Steadfast LA

The organization reflects Caruso's broader policy approach emphasizing private-sector efficiency and reduced bureaucracy. Steadfast LA is a civic nonprofit founded by Rick Caruso and led by the private sector, dedicated to helping Los Angeles rebuild faster, stronger, and more resilient after disaster by reducing red tape, bringing together top leaders and bold ideas, and mobilizing private expertise. Key initiatives include modular housing for vulnerable populations, AI-driven permitting systems to streamline approval processes, and infrastructure improvements like undergrounding power lines.

## Political Donations and Financial Contributions

Beyond Steadfast LA, Caruso has been a significant political donor across the spectrum. Most notably, Caruso has dropped about $1.6 million on his choice for governor, with another $250,000 going to an independent expenditure committee backing San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan's bid for the governorship. A complaint accuses Mahan and billionaire PAC donors Michael Moritz and Rick Caruso, among others, of flouting state laws that prohibit "independent expenditure" committees from coordinating with candidates.

Earlier in his career, Caruso made substantial political donations related to his business interests. Caruso has lavished donations on elected officials with the power to approve his luxury residential and retail projects, with some of his larger donations coming in 2016, just before the City Council voted to approve a zone change and other concessions he requested for his luxury retail and residential tower. In 2016, the Los Angeles Times reported that Caruso, his affiliates and family had given more than $476,000 to L.A. city officials over the past five years.

## Ideological Alignments and Values

Caruso's political giving reflects alignment with Catholic social teaching and business-oriented governance. In 2008, he donated $6 million to fund the $33 million construction of the USC Caruso Catholic Center, and in 2017, Caruso was given the Knight Commander honor in the Order of St. Gregory by Pope Francis. Additionally, Caruso has stated on his campaign website that "We need tougher laws around transparency and reporting that reform the way development is approved in Los Angeles", though critics have noted contradictions between such stated positions and his historical political contributions.

claude.question: How has Rick Caruso's reputation and public perception evolved across different demographic and geographic communities in Los Angeles based on recent media coverage and polling data?
claude.answer:
Based on recent media coverage and polling data, Rick Caruso's reputation and public perception across Los Angeles communities shows significant geographic and demographic variation, reflecting deep political divides within the city.

## Geographic Performance and Conservative Strongholds

Conservative LA neighborhoods handed Rick Caruso a near-majority (about 49%) in the 2022 mayoral primary, against just 37% citywide. More specifically, Caruso gave 56% in the last mayor's race among wealthy Westside moderate neighborhoods including Brentwood, Pacific Palisades, and areas around Beverly Hills, which comprise some of the most affluent and educated demographics in the city. This demonstrates strong support among upscale, affluent communities contrasting sharply with his broader citywide performance.

In the 2022 general election, Bass defeated Caruso 54.8% to 45.2%, indicating that despite his strength in conservative and wealthy precincts, he faced significant headwinds citywide. Even with his late conversion to the city's dominant political party, some observers believe Caruso might have had a chance in the mayor's race but for the Supreme Court's Dobbs ruling just as the campaign was heating up, which stripped women of a federal right to an abortion and reminded voters of sharp differences between parties on fundamental rights, and it did not help Caruso that he had contributed to candidates who opposed abortion rights.

## Political Positioning and City-Wide Perception

There is an analysis that Los Angeles is a very liberal city, and Caruso's moderate politics—either in his previous life as a Republican or his current iteration as a Democrat—are simply a non-sale in Los Angeles. This fundamental ideological mismatch appears to be a persistent structural constraint on Caruso's appeal across much of the city's electorate.

## Recent Developments and Reputation Shifts

In early 2026, Caruso's reputation showed signs of potential resurgence linked to current governance issues. Businessman Rick Caruso, who last month announced that he would not seek elected office after an unsuccessful run against Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass, indicated this week that he is reconsidering in light of new allegations that Bass pushed for a report on the Palisades Fire to be watered down. However, Caruso announced he would not run for mayor or governor in 2026, despite speculation and controversy surrounding Mayor Bass, and decided against running for mayor or governor, citing family considerations and disappointment, which caused a sharp drop in his market price and reshaped the race.

In summary, Caruso's reputation in Los Angeles remains highly stratified by geography and demographic factors. He enjoys substantial support among affluent, conservative-leaning neighborhoods on the Westside and San Fernando Valley, but struggles significantly with the broader, more liberal Los Angeles electorate—a gap that appears difficult to bridge given the city's leftward political trajectory and demographic composition.
